cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Looking for a spark of inspiration? Spark60 is your monthly passport to big ideas and bestselling thinkers. In this edition, hear from Oliver Burkeman .
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the sculpture garden, drift through the contemporary galleries, and take your time — the Triton punches way above its weight for a free museum and the outdoor space is perfect on a mild day like today.
cards.why: It's free, it's in Santa Clara, and it's genuinely worth two hours of your afternoon.

cards.name: Swana Sweets at Student Union
cards.blurb: Swing through the SJSU Student Union for Swana Sweets and check out what's on offer from this outdoor pop-up.
cards.why: Free, casual, and a great excuse to eat something good before the afternoon kicks in.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from the vendors — tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee, whatever you're feeling. Grab a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: The weather today is exactly right for sitting outside with good food and no agenda.

cards.name: Beat The Lock Escape Room
cards.address: 2131 The Alameda A, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a room at Beat The Lock and spend 60 minutes working through puzzles with your group — it's genuinely fun and gets competitive fast.
cards.why: A solid way to spend the early evening, and way more interesting than another lap around a mall.

cards.name: BE.STEAK.A
cards.address: 1887 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at BE.STEAK.A in Campbell for a proper dinner. This is a splurge spot, so go in ready to commit to the experience.
cards.why: After a full day you deserve a real sit-down dinner, and this one's worth it.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown with mochi from Okayama Kobo and a bowl of udon at Gombei — one of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and worth lingering in.
cards.why: Cool morning, walkable neighborhood, great food before the day really kicks off.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through King Library to check out the Patient No More Exhibition, a thought-provoking free display worth a solid hour of your morning.
cards.why: It's free, it's on your way through downtown, and it's the kind of thing you'd actually remember seeing.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and mix and match from a dozen vendors under one roof. Tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee — grab a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch stop in downtown San Jose and you can eat outside in this weather.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Municipal Rose Garden, one of the most beautiful free spots in the South Bay, especially in spring when everything is blooming.
cards.why: 74 degrees and roses in bloom is basically a perfect afternoon.

cards.name: Restful SPA
cards.address: 991 Saratoga Ave #150, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Treat yourself to a couple hours at Restful SPA on Saratoga Ave. Good way to unwind before a night out at the opera.
cards.why: You've got time to kill before the evening and showing up relaxed to a show beats killing time scrolling.

cards.name: Loto-Ja Ice Cream
cards.address: 74 S 24th St, San Jose, CA 95116, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Loto-Ja Ice Cream for a scoop before the evening gets going. Fun flavors, under $15, and a low-key way to ease into the night.
cards.why: Light pre-show stop that won't weigh you down before sitting in a theater.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre Center for SJSU Opera Theatre's Double Bill of La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator, two dramatically different one-act operas on one stage.
cards.why: This is genuinely a cool pairing and student opera productions at SJSU are legitimately great.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Prompted by a true story, this sung-through opera follows a stuck Chinese food deliveryman through 81 hours trapped in a Bronx elevator. This haunting 81-minute musical hybrid transforms what it…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Greenlee's Bakery
cards.address: 1081 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a pastry and coffee from this beloved neighborhood bakery on The Alameda — grab something fresh from the case and eat it right there.
cards.why: Perfect low-key way to ease into a Thursday morning before your volunteer shift.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Head to the Branch Buddy volunteer session and help clear library fines for community members — it's a feel-good hour that actually makes a difference.
cards.why: You locked it in for a reason, and it's a genuinely good thing to do with your morning.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and graze your way through the vendors — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a patio seat if the sun's out.
cards.why: 74 degrees and a covered patio downtown is hard to beat for a Thursday lunch.

cards.name: Volunteer Away Your Fines as a Branch Buddy
cards.blurb: Every now and then, libraries need a little extra love from the communities they serve. Volunteer away your library fines at a rate of $20 per hour while helping the library maintain a clean and…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Swing by the Thompson Gallery to check out 'Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology' — it's a thought-provoking show about how tech is reshaping the way we communicate, with work that'll stick with you.
cards.why: Free art right after your volunteer slot and it fits the thoughtful morning vibe perfectly.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along the Guadalupe River trail. It cuts right through downtown and is way more peaceful than you'd expect for being in the middle of the city.
cards.why: The weather is dialed in today and this is a genuinely nice way to spend an early afternoon.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden, then take your time through the contemporary galleries inside.
cards.why: It's free, it's calm, and the sculpture garden on a 74-degree afternoon is genuinely lovely.

cards.name: Suspiro
cards.address: 3060 Olsen Dr Suite 20, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Suspiro for dinner. It's a step up from casual so dress a little, sit back, and enjoy the food without rushing.
cards.why: You earned a proper sit-down dinner after a full day of good stuff.

cards.name: Voyagers Book Club Grades 3 and 4
cards.blurb: Voyagers Book Club will feature a book just for currently enrolled 3rd and 4th graders. Please come to the Children's Reference Desk at Cupertino Library to sign up and get a book. Registration is…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Puffin Donuts
cards.address: 1442 Pollard Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Puffin Donuts — grab a couple of their fresh-made donuts and a coffee before the day really kicks off.
cards.why: A local favorite worth the detour, and you'll be glad you didn't skip breakfast.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Catch Jessica Monette's exhibition at SCU's Project Room — immersive mixed-media work exploring roots, grief, and transformation. Give yourself the full two hours to sit with it.
cards.why: Free contemporary art in a university gallery setting is a genuinely good Thursday morning move.

cards.name: Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Drop into the Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness session at the Cupertino library — guided, low-key, and a genuinely good reset for the middle of the week.
cards.why: Free, no experience needed, and 74 degrees later makes it the ideal wind-down before a relaxed evening.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head into the Triton Museum of Art for the permanent collection and rotating shows, then wander the outdoor sculpture garden while the weather is perfect.
cards.why: It's free, it's beautiful, and most people have no idea it exists.

cards.name: Mio Vicino
cards.address: 1290 Benton St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Mio Vicino, a cozy Italian spot in Santa Clara. Order something pasta-forward and take your time.
cards.why: Solid mid-range Italian that hits the spot after a morning of art.

cards.name: Star Thai Massage
cards.address: 539 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Book a Thai massage at Star Thai Massage on S Murphy Ave and actually decompress for an hour or more.
cards.why: After a full day of moving around, this is exactly what you'll want.

cards.name: Moods. Wine Bar and Bistro
cards.address: 111 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at Moods Wine Bar and Bistro. Grab a table, order a bottle, and graze through the menu at whatever pace feels right.
cards.why: A 4.9-rated wine bar with a great atmosphere is the perfect Thursday night closer.

cards.name: Ada's Cafe
cards.address: 3700 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Ada's Cafe with a coffee and something from the menu — it's a low-key, unpretentious spot that doesn't try too hard.
cards.why: Good food at under $15 a head is a perfect way to kick off a Thursday.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Swing by the Friends of the Library Preview Sale before Chess Time — it's free, and the friend preview sales always have the best picks before the public gets in.
cards.why: Free book sale with early access is worth a quick stop.

cards.name: Chess Time
cards.blurb: Casual chess for all skill levels. Grades K-12.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Computer History Museum and lose yourself in the exhibits. The stories behind early computing are genuinely wild, even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: It's one of the better museums in the whole Bay Area and the afternoon is perfect for it.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Take a walk along Shoreline Park and Lake while the afternoon warms up to that 74-degree high. Lap the lake or just sit by the water and decompress.
cards.why: The weather today is basically made for this.

cards.name: FireWorks Ceramics
cards.address: 483 Hope St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into FireWorks Ceramics on Hope Street and see what's happening at the studio. It's a working ceramics space with a creative, unhurried vibe.
cards.why: A nice way to wind down the afternoon before dinner.

cards.name: Moods. Wine Bar and Bistro
cards.address: 111 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Moods Wine Bar and Bistro in Sunnyvale. Settle into a table, order a glass of something good, and take your time with dinner.
cards.why: A 4.9-rated wine bar on a Thursday night is exactly how you close out a solid day.

cards.name: Driftwood Deli & Market
cards.address: 3450 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a coffee and something to eat at this neighborhood deli before the day gets going — solid, no-fuss, exactly what a morning needs.
cards.why: A low-key way to start that won't eat into your day.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hike the Stanford Foothills loop up to The Dish — the views from the top are genuinely worth the climb, and 63°F is perfect hiking weather.
cards.why: Cool morning temps and clear skies make this the ideal window to get outside.

cards.name: Ccrma Presents: MediaFlock 2026 – Multimedia Art Festival
cards.address: 660 Lomita Court, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Swing through the MediaFlock Multimedia Art Festival at CCRMA — it's a mix of sound art, visual installations, and experimental work from artists pushing what media can do.
cards.why: Free arts festival on a Thursday is a genuine treat and worth carving out time for.

cards.name: Anton
cards.address: 660 Stanford Shopping Center, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Anton at Stanford Shopping Center for lunch — the menu leans California fresh and the patio is great on a day like this.
cards.why: One of the better sit-down lunch spots in Palo Alto and a solid mid-day reset.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch '1,000 Ways to Hold' by Erika Chong Shuch at the Anderson Collection building — it's a new performance work that's worth seeing while it's here.
cards.why: Free contemporary performance art at Stanford doesn't come around all the time.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Computer History Museum and dig into the full sweep of computing history — the exhibits go deeper than you'd expect and there's always something that'll stop you in your tracks.
cards.why: Two hours here goes fast in the best way, and it closes at 5 so get there before the doors shut.

cards.name: Vin Vino Wine
cards.address: 437 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Vin Vino Wine on California Ave — grab a glass or two and unwind after a full day.
cards.why: A 4.9-rated wine bar is the right way to close out a Thursday.

cards.name: The Ecological Developmental Biology Symposium
cards.address: 120 Ocean View Blvd Pacific Grove, CA 93950
cards.blurb: Join us for the new inaugural Eco-Evo-Devo Summit, an exciting examination of how biological systems adapt to the fast changing environment. Hear keynotes, Stanford experts, and poster findings from…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Angelicakes
cards.address: 1849 Little Orchard St, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Angelicakes with something fresh and sweet — great pastries and coffee to ease into the day.
cards.why: A chill, local spot to kick things off before the day gets going.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Swing by the Warehouse Gallery at Santa Clara University and take in Jessica Monette's exhibition exploring themes of roots, grief, and becoming. It's quiet, thoughtful, and free.
cards.why: Free contemporary art on a beautiful spring morning is a pretty solid way to spend an hour.

cards.name: Island Taste Caribbean Grill
cards.address: 225 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Island Taste Caribbean Grill on E Santa Clara St. Order whatever's hot and don't overthink it.
cards.why: Affordable, delicious, and a nice change of pace from the usual downtown lunch spots.

cards.name: Sciencepalooza! 2026 at Duncan Hall
cards.blurb: Join us on April 11, 2026 in Duncan Hall for a fun-filled, hands-on STEM event that’s perfect for kids AND parents from 11 a.m to 3 pm. Explore cool STEM activities, learn about STEM careers, and…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Head to King Library for the Patient No More Exhibition — a powerful collection exploring disability rights and activism that's worth slowing down for.
cards.why: It's free, it's thought-provoking, and it's a great way to fill the late morning.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the San Jose Municipal Rose Garden. April is when this place really pops, so take your time wandering the paths and actually stop to smell a few.
cards.why: Spring blooms, perfect 74-degree weather, and it costs nothing.

cards.name: Dumont Creamery & Café
cards.address: 28 N Almaden Ave Suite 40, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Dumont Creamery and Café for an afternoon treat. Get a scoop or two and sit with it.
cards.why: Warm afternoon, great ice cream. Easy call.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head out to Excite Ballpark for the San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide. Grab a beer, find your seats, and settle into a proper Thursday night baseball game.
cards.why: Minor league baseball on a warm spring evening is genuinely hard to beat for under $20 a head.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee Co.
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #10, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Chromatic Coffee Co. with a well-crafted espresso drink and something to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: One of the better third-wave coffee spots in San Jose and a solid way to start a Thursday.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: The Patient No More Exhibition at King Library is free and worth a walk-through. Take your time with it.
cards.why: Free art on a weekday morning is hard to beat.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and graze your way through the vendors. Tacos, banh mi, ramen — pick whatever sounds good and grab a patio seat if the sun cooperates.
cards.why: It's the easiest, most satisfying lunch call in downtown San Jose.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along Guadalupe River Park. It threads right through downtown and is way more peaceful than it has any right to be.
cards.why: Perfect afternoon weather for a casual stroll with no agenda.

cards.name: Little Wine House
cards.address: 350 W Julian St Bldg 4, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Little Wine House is a five-star spot on Julian Street. Pull up a seat, work through a glass or two, and let the afternoon settle.
cards.why: You're adults with nowhere to be on a Thursday afternoon — lean into it.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide at Excite Ballpark. Grab a beer and a dog, find good seats, and enjoy some minor league baseball under the lights.
cards.why: Minor league games have a relaxed vibe that's genuinely fun for adults, especially on a warm spring evening.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 3990 El Camino Real unit c, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid cup at ZombieRunner Coffee, a quirky Palo Alto spot that's part running store, part cafe — grab your drink and settle in before the day picks up.
cards.why: Good coffee and a laid-back vibe is the right way to kick off a Thursday.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits are genuinely wild, covering everything from ancient calculating machines to the internet age, and it never feels like homework.
cards.why: It's the kind of place that surprises you even if you think you're not a tech person.

cards.name: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
cards.blurb: Text from presenter: Join wildlife photographer Joan Sparks on a journey to discover elegant, long-legged wading birds whose ancestors appear in the fossil record dating back 50 million years. Be it…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Satura Cakes
cards.address: 200 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Satura Cakes on Main Street in Los Altos is the move for a late lunch or afternoon treat — grab a slice of cake, a sandwich, or both, and sit outside if the patio's open.
cards.why: You're already in Los Altos and this place is genuinely worth stopping for.

cards.name: Gourmet Works
cards.address: 334 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Gourmet Works on Main Street for a browse — it's a well-curated kitchen and cooking store where it's easy to spend an hour just picking things up and putting them down.
cards.why: It's a fun place to wander without any real agenda.

cards.name: Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto
cards.address: 368 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage or bodywork session at Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto and let the afternoon melt away before dinner.
cards.why: A weekday afternoon slot is the best time to actually get in and fully decompress.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar @ Oklava
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Kenz Coffee Bar, a cozy spot on University Ave — grab a coffee and settle in before the day gets going.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into a Thursday with good coffee in the heart of Palo Alto.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Stanford campus to Cantor Arts Center and spend some time with the Rodin sculpture garden outside and rotating exhibits inside — it's genuinely world-class for a free museum.
cards.why: Free art on a beautiful spring morning is hard to beat.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Swing by 314 Lomita Dr to catch Erika Chong Shuch's new work before your noon commitment — it's a short walk from Cantor and completely free.
cards.why: It's a rare chance to see original performance art on campus with zero barrier to entry.

cards.name: Bell's Books
cards.address: 536 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Graphic Novel Book Club time — bring your copy, grab a seat, and dig into the discussion with fellow readers.
cards.why: This is the anchor of your day and a great excuse to be social on a weekday.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Monthly book discussion on a graphic novel selected by the group! This program is for readers age 16 and up. 2025-2026 titles: September 11: Understanding Comics by Scott McCloud (1994), 224 pages…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Bell's Books
cards.address: 536 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: After book club, head to Bell's Books on Emerson for some browsing — it's a beloved independent bookstore with deep shelves and a wonderfully old-school atmosphere.
cards.why: Post-book-club energy is basically made for wandering a used bookstore.

cards.name: Urfa Bistro
cards.address: 233 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Urfa Bistro for a Turkish lunch — order the mezze spreads and something off the grill and take your time with it.
cards.why: You need a real lunch after the book club and this spot is worth it.

cards.name: Bell's Books
cards.address: 536 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Urfa Bistro in Los Altos, a Turkish spot worth the trip — go for the kebabs and mezze and take your time.
cards.why: You need a proper sit-down lunch and this place consistently delivers.

cards.name: Pearson/Arastradero Preserve
cards.address: 1530 Arastradero Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Pearson/Arastradero Preserve for an afternoon walk through the rolling hills and open grassland — the trails are well-marked and the views open up nicely.
cards.why: 73 degrees and sunny is exactly the kind of afternoon you want to spend outside.

cards.name: Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto
cards.address: 368 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto — book ahead if you can, and let the post-hike soreness melt away.
cards.why: After an afternoon on the trails, this is exactly the right move.

cards.name: State of Mind Public House and Pizzeria
cards.address: 101 Plaza N, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night with dinner at State of Mind Public House and Pizzeria in Los Altos — get a craft beer and a pizza and camp out at the bar or patio.
cards.why: Good pizza, good beer, and a relaxed vibe is the perfect Thursday night ending.

cards.name: Craft Social
cards.blurb: Bring a craft you're working on, or something you'd like to share with the group.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: GK Pastry & Sourdough
cards.address: 251 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at GK Pastry & Sourdough — grab a flaky pastry and a coffee and ease into the day.
cards.why: Los Altos has a good walkable main street vibe and this is a legit local bakery worth the trip.

cards.name: American Cancer Society Discovery Shop - Los Altos, CA
cards.address: 243 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into the American Cancer Society Discovery Shop right on Main St — it's a well-curated thrift spot with books, clothing, and random gems you won't expect.
cards.why: Good cause, great finds, and you're already on the block.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a solid two hours at the Computer History Museum — the timeline of computing exhibit alone is worth it, and the hands-on stuff keeps it from feeling like a lecture.
cards.why: Even if you're not a tech person, the stories here are genuinely wild and the museum is huge enough to fill a morning.

cards.name: My Year With AI
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Catch My Year With AI inside the museum — it's a focused exhibit exploring how one person lived and worked alongside AI tools for a full year.
cards.why: You're already there, and this one adds a personal angle that makes the bigger museum themes stick.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head to Shoreline Park for a walk along the water — the loop around the lake is flat and easy, and 74°F is basically perfect for it.
cards.why: You need some fresh air after a museum morning and this is one of the better spots on the Bay to just decompress.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar @ Oklava
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Kenz Coffee Bar, grab a great cup and settle in for a bit before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into a Thursday with good coffee on University Ave.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Stanford campus to Cantor Arts Center, spend time with the permanent collection and don't skip the Rodin sculpture garden out front.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a mild morning is a genuinely good use of two hours.

cards.name: Ethel's Fancy
cards.address: 550 Waverley St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Ethel's Fancy for lunch, it's a creative, ingredient-forward spot worth lingering over.
cards.why: Solid lunch pick that won't leave you in a food coma heading into the afternoon.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Take the Stanford Ave trailhead up The Dish Trail and do the full loop for sweeping views of the Bay and the foothills.
cards.why: 73°F and sunny is exactly the weather this hike was made for.

cards.name: SWEET55 - Swiss Chocolates & Confections
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suite 123, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into SWEET55 for Swiss chocolates and confections, grab a few pieces to snack on and something to take home.
cards.why: A post-hike treat stop that actually delivers something special.

cards.name: Serenity Wellness Spa
cards.address: 200 California Ave Suite 190, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book an evening treatment at Serenity Wellness Spa and let the post-hike soreness melt away.
cards.why: After a few miles on the trail, this is the obvious move.

cards.name: Ccrma Presents: MediaFlock 2026 – Multimedia Art Festival
cards.address: 660 Lomita Court, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: MediaFlock 2026 is a one-day art festival featuring multimedia art pieces from CCRMA students and guest artists from other California universities. Join us on April 10th to celebrate 30+ pieces of…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman live at SCU talking about his book Meditations for Mortals — it's a genuinely refreshing take on limits, productivity, and just being a human who can't do everything.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a perfect way to kick off a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton Museum of Art and spend time in the sculpture garden while the morning light is still soft. The contemporary exhibitions rotate often so there's usually something worth sitting with for a minute.
cards.why: It's free, underrated, and one of the most pleasant indoor-outdoor combos in Santa Clara.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Head over to the Market Street Spring Jubilee for live music and that classic outdoor festival vibe. Grab something to eat from the vendors while you're there and just hang for a bit before your 2 PM.
cards.why: Free live music on a 72-degree afternoon is hard to say no to.

cards.name: Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Join the AI-Powered Tools session to level up how you use AI for research and searching. Practical, hands-on, and the kind of thing that actually changes how you work day to day.
cards.why: This is the locked item for the day and it fits perfectly into the afternoon flow.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: *]:pointer-events-auto scroll-mt-[calc(var(--header-height)+min(200px,max(70px,20svh)))]" dir="auto" data-turn-id="request-WEB:3a0652f0-f427-46fa-8464-37c0fbcaabc6-0"…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market for dinner. Pick a couple of different vendors, grab a table on the patio, and mix and match whatever sounds good, whether that's tacos, banh mi, or ramen.
cards.why: It's the most flexible dinner spot in the area and the patio is ideal on an evening like this.

cards.name: Beat The Lock Escape Room
cards.address: 2131 The Alameda A, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night with an escape room at Beat The Lock. Book a room in advance if you can, and go in expecting to get genuinely stumped at least once.
cards.why: It's a solid way to cap a full day on a high-energy, laugh-a-lot note.

cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Downtown
cards.address: 340 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Los Gatos Cafe Downtown with a proper sit-down breakfast. Good coffee, relaxed vibe, and a solid way to ease into the day.
cards.why: Perfect weather to grab a patio table and wake up slowly before the day kicks off.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail and enjoy the shaded path along the creek. Flat and easy, so you can actually talk and not just huff up a hill.
cards.why: 66 degrees and a creek trail is basically a gift on a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Aqui Campbell
cards.address: 201 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Aqui Campbell for fresh Cal-Mex food in a laid-back setting. Order whatever looks good on the board and sit outside if you can.
cards.why: Solid lunch spot that won't leave you dragging through the afternoon.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Spend the early afternoon wandering Hakone Estate and Gardens in Saratoga. The Japanese garden terraces are genuinely peaceful, and the bamboo grove alone is worth the visit.
cards.why: You've got a free afternoon before the evening workshop and this is the best possible use of it.

cards.name: Saratoga Meat & Fish
cards.address: 14320 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd #5, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Saratoga Meat and Fish to browse what's fresh. Great spot to pick up something for the weekend if anything catches your eye.
cards.why: It's a fun browse even if you don't buy anything, and it's right in Saratoga before your evening.

cards.name: Sascc & County Assessor: Property Assessment Workshop
cards.blurb: Head to Sascc & County Assessor: Property Assessment Workshop and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: West Valley College, Fox 120 Lecture Hall

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman live talking about his book Meditations for Mortals — it's a genuinely interesting conversation about limits, productivity, and just being a person. Free, and a great way to kick off a Thursday morning.
cards.why: Starting the day with something thought-provoking beats scrolling over coffee.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton's current exhibitions and spend time in the sculpture garden out back. It's free, genuinely good contemporary art, and never crowded on a weekday.
cards.why: Perfect follow-up to a morning talk about ideas — keeps the creative brain going.

cards.name: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
cards.blurb: Head over to Los Altos for this guided walk focused on pelicans, herons, and egrets. The naturalist-led format means you'll actually learn to tell the birds apart, not just squint at the water.
cards.why: 74°F and sunny is ideal for a midday walk with good company.

cards.name: Fish With You鱼你在一起Mountain View
cards.address: 156 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Fish With You on Castro St. Order whatever the fish-forward specials are and eat in the casual dining room or take it to a table outside if the patio's open.
cards.why: Castro St is a solid lunch strip and this spot delivers every time.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Computer History Museum. The Revolution exhibit alone takes a solid hour, and the punch card and early personal computing sections are genuinely wild to walk through in 2025.
cards.why: Even if tech isn't your thing, the sheer scale of how fast everything changed is kind of mind-blowing.

cards.name: Siam Pavilion Thai Massage
cards.address: 830 Stewart Dr STE 172, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Siam Pavilion for a Thai massage to cap the afternoon. It's a legit, no-frills spot with skilled therapists. Walk out feeling like a new person before dinner.
cards.why: After a full day of walking and exploring, this is exactly what your legs need.

cards.name: CUANYUE MALATANG (Sunnyvale)
cards.address: 150 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at CUANYUE MALATANG in Sunnyvale. Pick your proteins and veggies, choose your spice level, and let them cook it up in the rich mala broth. It's customizable, warming, and genuinely delicious.
cards.why: Malatang is the perfect dinner after a long day and this Sunnyvale spot is hard to beat.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown — grab mochi or a pastry at Okayama Kobo, pick up coffee, and wander Jackson Street at a relaxed pace.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a great way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Join Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Porcelain Casting Workshop at Art & Design Building
cards.blurb: Drop into this free porcelain casting workshop at SJSU's Art & Design Building and try your hand at the craft alongside working artists.
cards.why: A free hands-on art workshop on a Thursday morning is genuinely hard to beat.

cards.name: Swana Sweets at Student Union
cards.blurb: Swing by the Student Union for Swana Sweets — free treats and a lively outdoor vibe right on campus.
cards.why: Free food, good weather, and a fun campus atmosphere make this an easy lunch-hour stop.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from the vendors — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: 76 degrees and a covered patio with a dozen food options is a pretty perfect lunch situation.

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Slingshot Pinball on Lincoln Ave for a couple hours of machines. It's a serious collection, not a bar afterthought, so you can actually dig in.
cards.why: Great way to fill the afternoon before the evening kicks off.

cards.name: Join Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Porcelain Casting Workshop at Art & Design Building
cards.blurb: GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting — show up, see what the local drone and mapping community is building, and meet some fellow enthusiasts.
cards.why: This is the anchor of the evening and a genuinely cool niche community to tap into.

cards.name: Smash City Rage Rooms
cards.address: 858 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Work out any leftover stress at Smash City — grab a crate and wreck some stuff before the club meeting.
cards.why: It's a legitimately fun pre-evening activity and you'll walk into that 5 PM meeting feeling great.

cards.name: GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting
cards.blurb: We hope you had a great spring break! We are happy to announce the next GIS & Drone Society meeting is this Thursday, April 9 at 5 pm in WSQ 115. We will continue reviewing study material for the…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Santana Row. Walk the boulevard, browse a few shops, then grab dinner at Straits, Sino, or settle onto the Zinnia patio with a drink.
cards.why: A warm April evening on Santana Row is exactly what this weather was made for.

cards.name: Voyager Craft Coffee
cards.address: 111 W St John St Suite 100, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Voyager Craft Coffee with a well-pulled espresso or a pour-over and ease into the day.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone and this place does it right.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton Museum of Art's galleries and spend time in the sculpture garden while the weather is still cool and pleasant.
cards.why: Free world-class contemporary art with an outdoor sculpture garden on a 72-degree morning is hard to beat.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU for some mind-bending visual art before lunch.
cards.why: It's free, it's today, and it's a genuinely interesting way to spend an hour.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market and mix and match lunch from the vendors — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good — then grab a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the best all-in-one food stop in the South Bay and you've got plenty of time to enjoy it.

cards.name: The Sense USA_ Santa Clara
cards.address: 1841 Pruneridge Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at The Sense USA in Santa Clara and decompress for a couple hours before the evening kicks off.
cards.why: Getting a treatment in before a stimulating evening talk is a solid move.

cards.name: Student Art Showcase at King Library
cards.blurb: Catch the Student Art Showcase at King Library for a quick browse through student work on your way to the evening event.
cards.why: It's free student art and a nice low-key way to fill the gap before 5 PM.

cards.name: Black Corporate Board Readiness Program (BCBR C16) In Session
cards.blurb: Attend the Ruha Benjamin talk on Race, Technology, and Social Justice at Santa Clara University — this is the centerpiece of the evening.
cards.why: Ruha Benjamin's work on algorithmic bias is sharp, provocative, and genuinely worth showing up for.

cards.name: Ruha Benjamin: Race, Technology, and Social Justice
cards.blurb: Ruha Benjamin is the Alexander Stewart 1886 Professor of African American studies at Princeton University, where she specializes in the interdisciplinary study of science, medicine, and technology…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Antoine’s Cookie Shop Los Gatos
cards.address: 35 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a cookie (yes, for breakfast — no regrets) or grab whatever's fresh out of the oven at this Los Gatos gem with a near-perfect reputation.
cards.why: Best way to kick off a Thursday is with something sweet and a little indulgent.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail and let the morning air do its thing. It's shaded, easy, and runs along the creek so the whole thing feels like a reset.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny is exactly the weather this trail was made for.

cards.name: Jake’s of Saratoga
cards.address: 12175 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd Suite"E, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Jake's of Saratoga for lunch. It's a solid neighborhood spot with a comfortable vibe, great for settling in over a proper midday meal.
cards.why: After a morning on your feet, a sit-down lunch in Saratoga hits just right.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander through Hakone Estate and Gardens, one of the few authentic Japanese gardens on the West Coast. Take your time on the bamboo paths and just breathe it in.
cards.why: It's genuinely hard to believe a place this peaceful exists in the middle of Silicon Valley.

cards.name: San Francisco Shakespeare Festival Presents Julius Caesar
cards.blurb: Catch the San Francisco Shakespeare Festival performing Julius Caesar at the Cupertino library. Free outdoor Shakespeare on a warm afternoon is a rare thing.
cards.why: You'll be glad you went — this kind of free performance doesn't come around often.

cards.name: Young & Bloom Skin
cards.address: 130 Excelsior Ave Ste 106, Saratoga Springs, NY 12866, USA
cards.blurb: Take in the Young Artists of Art Students' Atelier Exhibit and see what the next generation of painters and sculptors is putting out. Walk slowly and actually look.
cards.why: Local student art shows always surprise you with how good they are.

cards.name: Young Artists of Art Students' Atelier Exhibit
cards.blurb: This is an exhibit of drawings and paintings by young local artists who are current and former students of Gabriel Coke from Saratoga, California. Photo Credit: Courtesy of Gabriel Coke. **This…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: El Guapo's
cards.address: 266 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at El Guapo's in Campbell with tacos and whatever catches your eye on the menu. Casual, delicious, and easy on the wallet.
cards.why: A 4.5-rated taco spot under $15 a head is the perfect Thursday night closer.

cards.name: Peet's Coffee
cards.address: 2035 Camden Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a solid cup of coffee and something to eat before the day picks up.
cards.why: Every good Thursday needs a proper morning kickoff.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the contemporary exhibits and spend some time in the sculpture garden while the morning light is still soft — it's one of those places where you end up staying longer than planned.
cards.why: Free admission and genuinely interesting art make this an easy yes on a weekday morning.

cards.name: Paesano Ristorante Italiano
cards.address: 350 W Julian St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for a proper Italian lunch, something pasta or a classic secondi, at this West Julian spot that's been doing it right for years.
cards.why: You'll want a real sit-down meal before an evening of poetry and spoken word.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the trails through the creek canyon and soak up the afternoon in the foothills before everything closes up at five.
cards.why: 74 degrees and a wooded park is basically a perfect Thursday afternoon combination.

cards.name: Dumont Creamery & Café
cards.address: 28 N Almaden Ave Suite 40, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a scoop or a coffee drink at this downtown spot to tide you over before the show.
cards.why: It's a quick, easy way to decompress between the park and the evening.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Saul Williams, Derrick Sanderlin, and Ramona Webb on the same stage for the Tiny Room Poetry Series, this is a rare lineup of voices that hit hard whether you know their work or are coming in fresh.
cards.why: Spoken word at this level is genuinely worth clearing your evening for.

cards.name: Tiny Room Poetry Series with Saul Williams, Derrick Sanderlin & Ramona Webb — San Jose Jazz
cards.address: 310 South First St, San Jose
cards.blurb: We're proud to partner with Listen Different and Poetry Center San José for a special Tiny Room Poetry Series featuring seminal poet Saul Williams with Ramona Webb and Derrick Sanderlin.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the morning walking through the full exhibit hall at the Computer History Museum. The collection goes way deeper than you'd expect, from the earliest mainframes to the rise of the internet.
cards.why: Perfect way to kick off a Thursday when the crowds are light and the galleries are all yours.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head out to Shoreline for a walk along the lake with the bay views opening up behind you. Great weather today, so just wander and soak it in.
cards.why: 74 degrees with no rain is basically a gift, and this is the best spot to use it.

cards.name: NoodlePanda Restaurant Sunnyvale
cards.address: 1161 Lawrence Expy, Sunnyvale, CA 94089, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at NoodlePanda in Sunnyvale. Try the local favorite noodle bowls and settle in for a proper sit-down meal.
cards.why: Solid lunch spot that consistently delivers on flavor.

cards.name: Siam Pavilion Thai Massage
cards.address: 830 Stewart Dr STE 172, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Siam Pavilion Thai Massage and let the afternoon slow way down. A solid mid-day massage is one of those things you keep meaning to do.
cards.why: You've got a gap before the 4:30 event and this fills it better than anything else could.

cards.name: Steve Jobs in Exile
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Swing through the Steve Jobs in Exile exhibit at the Computer History Museum. It covers the wild years between Apple and Pixar and hits differently if you just did the main hall this morning.
cards.why: It's a sharp, focused exhibit that doesn't overstay its welcome.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Catch the Earth Heroes Mission Recycle event in Mountain View, a fun and informative program worth dropping into.
cards.why: It's free, it's local, and it's a nice way to round out an already full day.

cards.name: Fabulous Friday: Earth Heroes - Mission Recycle
cards.blurb: Games, crafts, and activities to protect our planet. Grades K-6. STEM.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Moods. Wine Bar and Bistro
cards.address: 111 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Moods Wine Bar and Bistro in Sunnyvale for dinner. Order a glass of something good and take your time with the menu.
cards.why: After a full day, a wine bar with great food is exactly the right ending.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 3990 El Camino Real unit c, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a coffee at ZombieRunner, a cult-favorite spot that doubles as a running store — grab your drink and settle in before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a genuinely local spot you won't find anywhere else.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Join one of the Spotlight Tours at the Anderson Collection, where a docent walks you through highlights of the permanent collection and gives you real context for the art.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a great way to actually understand what you're looking at.

cards.name: Drop-In Drawing @ The Anderson
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Stay at the Anderson and drop into the free drawing session — no experience needed, just show up with curiosity and sketch alongside other visitors.
cards.why: It's the kind of low-key creative thing you'd never do on your own but always enjoy when you do.

cards.name: La Bodeguita del Medio
cards.address: 463 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Head to La Bodeguita del Medio on California Ave for lunch. Cuban food, good cocktails, and a lively patio that makes a Thursday afternoon feel like a Friday.
cards.why: The California Ave vibe is perfect for a midday reset.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch on The Dish Trail, a 3.5-mile loop through Stanford's rolling foothills with sweeping views of the Bay and, yes, a giant radio dish at the top.
cards.why: It's one of those hikes that actually delivers on the views people keep talking about.

cards.name: Wildseed
cards.address: South, 855 El Camino Real Building 4, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Wildseed on El Camino for an early pre-game bite or a drink at the bar. Plant-based and elegant, it's a great spot to fuel up before heading to the stadium.
cards.why: The food is genuinely impressive and the vibe is relaxed enough for a quick pre-game stop.

cards.name: Stanford Cardinal Baseball vs. Santa Clara Broncos Baseball
cards.address: 151 Sam McDonald Mall
cards.blurb: Head to Stanford Cardinal Baseball vs. Santa Clara Broncos Baseball and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Boast coffee
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a coffee at Boast — grab something hot and settle in before the day gets rolling.
cards.why: A strong cup of coffee makes everything that follows better.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Stanford Foothills trail and earn those views — the Dish loop is a solid 3.5-mile walk with sweeping South Bay panoramas the whole way up.
cards.why: 73°F with clear skies is basically the perfect hiking temperature.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop into this free performance piece by Erika Chong Shuch at the Anderson Collection building — it's a new work and exactly the kind of unexpected, thought-provoking thing you stumble into and talk about for weeks.
cards.why: Free, on campus, and genuinely different from anything else happening today.

cards.name: Learn English: Online Conversation Group
cards.blurb: Our ESL (English as a Second Language) / Learn English conversation group program is a friendly and informal environment for adults (18 years old and older) to practice speaking and listening in…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Seasons Noodles & Dumplings Garden
cards.address: 702 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Seasons Noodles and Dumplings Garden for lunch — order a bowl of noodles and a round of dumplings and take your time with it.
cards.why: You'll want a real meal after the morning hike.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — walk through the full arc of computing history from punch cards to AI, and don't skip the working Babbage Engine exhibit.
cards.why: The history here is genuinely wild and the exhibits hold up for hours.

cards.name: Blue Danube Spa
cards.address: 200 California Ave suite 170, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Blue Danube Spa in Palo Alto with a massage or treatment — let the morning hike fully leave your legs.
cards.why: A weekday afternoon is the best time to go; it's quiet and you can actually relax.

cards.name: Caffe Stellato
cards.address: 435 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Caffe Stellato on Emerson Street with dinner and a glass of wine on the patio — the evening air at 72°F is too good to waste indoors.
cards.why: A perfect end-of-day spot that doesn't require a reservation or a plan.
